title: Account access issue — dashboard/profile 404, settings accessible
author: DynaProx
body:
I'm having trouble accessing my account and would appreciate help figuring out what's going on.

What's happening:
- My dashboard page (dev.to/dashboard) shows the generic "Looks like this page doesn't exist or may not be published" 404 message.
- My public profile page also returns a 404.
- However, I can still log in and access Settings (dev.to/settings/account) normally.

This suggests my account still exists and I'm authenticated, but something is preventing the dashboard and profile from loading correctly.

Could you please check my account status and let me know:
1. Whether my account has been suspended, flagged, or restricted in any way
2. If so, what the reason is and what steps I can take to resolve it
3. If this is a bug on your end, when it might be fixed

title: What happened to the sidebar for Forem?
author: FrancisTRAlt
body: Not really a bug, but I notice a while ago where you were able to view different sub Forems inside of dev.to I was wondering what happened to that?

title: Back-fill articles.semantic_embedding across full DEV corpus
author: pkfrank
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

As a DEV admin who utilizes `articles.semantic_embedding` in reporting contexts, I'm currently limited to the set of articles with completed embeddings (not the full published archive). Coverage today is ~82k of ~2.3M published articles (~3.6%), and it's recency-skewed — embeddings are generated going forward on publish, so most of the historical corpus was never embedded.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Back-fill `articles.semantic_embedding` across the full published archive — a one-time, chunked, resumable sweep of the ~2.25M unembedded published articles, using the same embedding model/dimension as the existing column.

title: Posts not showing in order and some posts not showing up altogether
author: ShobanChiddarth
body:
<!-- Before creating a bug report, try disabling browser extensions to see if the bug is still present. -->

<!-- If you're having trouble updating your profile, it is likely because you logged in separately with GitHub & Twitter. Please check if this is the case before creating a bug report, and email support@dev.to so we can merge your accounts. -->

**Describe the bug**

My posts are not showing up on my profile. And sometimes it is not showing up in the order it was posted.

<!-- A clear and concise description of what the bug is. -->

**To Reproduce**

Visit: https://dev.to/shobanchiddarth

Scroll through the entire profile and see how many telegram related posts are being shown. Only one, the final one is being shown.

But I wrote 3 separate posts about Telegram so far under a series
 
1. https://dev.to/shobanchiddarth/how-to-circumvent-the-telegram-ban-in-india-36fl
2. https://dev.to/shobanchiddarth/deploying-a-telegram-mtproto-proxy-on-aws-with-terraform-25e5
3. https://dev.to/shobanchiddarth/telegram-ban-in-india-has-been-lifted-for-now-3g69

**Expected behavior**

When my profile is opened I want the posts to show up in the most recent to the least recent.

title: Summer Bug Smash challenge link opens a 404 Page Not Found
author: NandhuTee
body: **Describe the bug** Clicking the "DEV's Summer Bug Smash" challenge from the DEV Community Challenges page opens a "404 Page Not Found" page instead of the challenge details. **To Reproduce** 1. Open the DEV app. 2. Go to Community Challenges. 3. Tap "DEV's Summer Bug Smash". 4. The app displays a 404 Page Not Found page. **Expected behavior** The Summer Bug Smash challenge page should open correctly instead of showing a 404 error. **Screenshots** Attached screenshots showing the Community Challenges page and the 404 Page Not Found error. **Desktop :** - OS, version: N/A - Browser, version: N/A **Smartphone :** - Device: Motorola Edge 30 Fusion - OS, version: Android 14 - Browser, version: DEV Android app (latest version available from Google Play) **Additional context** The issue occurs when opening the Summer Bug Smash challenge from within the DEV Android app. Other pages appear to work normally.

title: Page views remain at 0 while reactions and comments are recorded
author: Luciano0322
body: **Describe the bug** The page views counter for one of my published posts remains at `0`, even though the post has already received reactions and comments. On the post dashboard, I can see: - Page Views: 0 - Reactions: 2 - Comments: 2 This seems inconsistent because the post is publicly accessible and users have already interacted with it. I also tried opening the post in an incognito window, but the page views counter still did not update after more than 24 hours. I am not sure whether this is a delayed analytics aggregation issue or a tracking/statistics bug, but the counter appears to be stuck at 0. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Publish a post on DEV. 2. Wait until the post receives reactions and/or comments. 3. Go to the post dashboard / statistics overview. 4. Observe that `Reactions` and `Comments` are recorded. 5. Observe that `Page Views` remains at `0`. 6. Open the post from an incognito window. 7. Wait for more than 24 hours. 8. Check the statistics again. 9. `Page Views` still remains at `0`. **Expected behavior** If a published post receives reactions and comments, the page views counter should eventually reflect at least some views after the analytics aggregation runs. Even if page views are delayed or sampled, I would expect the counter not to remain at `0` for more than 24 hours when the post has public interactions. **Screenshots** Attached screenshot shows the statistics overview where: - Page Views: 0 - Reactions: 2 - Comments: 2 **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS, version: Windows 11 - Browser, version: 149.0.7827.116 **Smartphone (please complete the following information):** - Device: Not tested - OS, version: Not tested - Browser, version: Not tested **Additional context** I have already tried opening the post from an incognito window. The post is accessible and has recorded reactions/comments, so it does not seem to be unpublished or private. This may be related specifically to the page view tracking or statistics aggregation pipeline.

title: Expanded code block hides exit control on touch/non-hover devices
author: anchildress1
body: **Describe the bug** When a code block is expanded into fullscreen mode, the exit control is hidden behind hover-only UI. The fullscreen state can appear to take over the page with no visible way to collapse it again, especially on touch devices or other non-hover contexts. **To Reproduce** 1. Go to an article with a fenced code block. 2. Click the fullscreen/expand control on the code block. 3. Observe the code block entering fullscreen mode. 4. Try to find a visible collapse/exit control without hovering the code block panel. **Expected behavior** Fullscreen code blocks should provide an always-visible, discoverable exit control. Users should not need hover behavior or prior knowledge of the `Escape` key to leave fullscreen mode. **Screenshots** **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS, version: - Browser, version: **Smartphone (please complete the following information):** - Device: Mac - OS, version: Tahoe 26.5.1 - Browser, version: Chrome Version 149.0.7827.197 (Official Build) (arm64) **Additional context** The current implementation generates both enter and exit icons in `.js-fullscreen-code-action`, then swaps icon visibility in fullscreen mode. However, `.highlight__panel` is hidden by default and only shown via `.highlight:hover .highlight__panel`, which makes the exit affordance easy to miss or unavailable on touch/non-hover devices. Related historical context: #8990 introduced the lightbox/fullscreen behavior, but this bug is specifically about exit affordance discoverability after expansion. Another edge case prevents use of escape key if you were to navigate back > forward in the browser. - OS, version: - Browser, version: **Smartphone (please complete the following information):** - Device: - OS, version: - Browser, version: **Additional context**

Set to Org instead of Personal **Expected behavior** No UI duplication **Screenshots** **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS, version: Windows 11 Pro x64 (10.0, build: 26200) - Browser, version: Chrome, Version 149.0.7827.199 (Official Build) (64-bit)

title: Unable to Create or Publish Posts on Dev.to
author: webcluesinfotec
body:
Hello Dev.to Support Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I am experiencing an issue with my Dev.to account. I am unable to create or publish articles because the "Create Post" option is not appearing on my account dashboard.

I have tried refreshing the page, logging out and back in, and using a different browser, but the issue still persists.

Could you please look into this issue and help me regain access to the post creation feature? If you need any additional information about my account, I would be happy to provide it.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Best regards,

title: dev.to api default image not render correctly in certain cases
author: FrancisTRAlt
body: **Describe the bug** When I use the dev.to api and try to get the "social_image", assuming I have no image cover for the article, some of my username is not render correctly. To be fair, I use special characters, which is expected to break. My username: **To Reproduce** Visit the dev.to api JSON in this specific case: https://dev.to/api/articles/devengers/honesty-is-the-best-policy-the-secret-wars-on-the-use-of-ai-on-dev-5606 Visit the image link that is labeled: `"social_image": "https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=1200,height=627,fit=cover,gravity=auto,format=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fotp9wutnggptuegtq4ws.png"` See that some of my username is not rendered. **Expected behavior** The username needs to be printed verbatim.
